blocks.content: you're staying in LA for break?
professor wants us to use the time. I have a project I need to actually start
something personal. still figuring out what it wants to be
I'll be home in Millhaven if you change your mind
He bought the ticket that night. Early morning flight into Atlanta. Eli picking him up.
He didn't tell his mom. He wanted her reaction on camera — real, unguarded, the way she was when she didn't know she was being seen.
He packed light. Camera. Extra batteries. The small tripod he carried everywhere. A clean shirt because Millhaven in October still required a clean shirt.

He thought about what he was going home to film and why. Every place that mattered. Every place that was theirs. He was going to put it all together and hand it to her across two thousand miles and ask her the question he'd been carrying since he was old enough to understand it.
His dad had left without explanation. No note that meant anything. Just — gone, one morning, like none of it had been real.

Kit had spent years watching his mom hold what was left. He'd spent years deciding, quietly, that when he chose someone it would be the last choice he ever made on the subject. No ambiguity. No half-measure. All the way in.
He zipped the bag. Set it by the door.
